Overview

Why This Project Matters

MRI scans are slow because they collect dense k-space data. By undersampling the raw signal and using ML to fill the gaps, scan times can be cut by 4–8× while maintaining diagnostic quality.

The Problem

Full MRI scans take 30–60 min. Patients move, artifacts appear, throughput is limited. Undersampling produces unusable images without ML.

Our Solution

Two containerized ML services: one reconstructs high-quality images from sparse data, another detects & segments brain tumors with clinical explanations.

Target Organ

Brain MRI — the most common neuroimaging modality. Segmentation covers necrotic core, peritumoral edema, and enhancing tumor regions.

Architecture

System Architecture

Raw k-space
.h5 / .dicom
→
Container 1
BasicUNet / MONAI
→
Reconstructed DICOM
→
Container 2
SegResNet / MONAI
→
Annotated NIfTI
+ Findings JSON

Container 1 — Reconstruction

Converts undersampled k-space to full-resolution DICOM. MONAI BasicUNet (7.8M params), pretrained on fastMRI T2.

POST /infer
In:  file_uri (.h5 or .dicom)
Out: reconstructed_dicom_uri

Container 2 — Detection

Segments brain tumors from reconstructed images. SegResNet (4.7M params), pretrained on BraTS 2018. Outputs segmentation + structured clinical findings.

POST /infer
In:  file_uri (.nii.gz)
Out: annotated_nifti_uri,
     findings_json_uri
UPLOADED
→
QUEUED
→
RECONSTRUCTING
→
DETECTING
→
COMPLETED
Model Selection

Why SegResNet, not SegFormer?

SegFormer

  • 2D transformer — processes slices independently
  • No volumetric context, misses inter-slice continuity
  • Designed for natural images (ADE20K, Cityscapes)
  • No pretrained weights for brain MRI

SegResNet ✓

  • 3D encoder-decoder with residual blocks
  • Full volumetric context across all planes
  • Pretrained on BraTS 2018 (brain tumors)
  • MONAI ecosystem, consistent with reconstruction
4.7M
Parameters
4ch
Input (T1c/T1/T2/FLAIR)
3ch
Output (TC/WT/ET)
[1,2,2,4]
Encoder Blocks
0.2
Dropout
Input Data

Four MRI Modalities

Each BraTS sample contains 4 co-registered MRI sequences at 1mm isotropic resolution

T1c

Post-contrast T1-weighted. Enhances active tumor with gadolinium.

T1

Pre-contrast T1-weighted. Anatomical reference, gray/white matter.

T2

T2-weighted. Highlights fluid, edema appears bright.

FLAIR

Fluid-attenuated. Suppresses CSF, best for edema visualization.

Ground Truth

Tumor Region Breakdown

Three pathological subregions annotated by expert neuroradiologists

Necrotic Core

Dead tissue at the tumor center. Label 1 in BraTS.

Peritumoral Edema

Swelling around the tumor. Label 2. Largest region by volume.

Enhancing Tumor

Active tumor with BBB breakdown. Label 3. Key grading indicator.

Data Pipeline

Data & Preprocessing

Dataset

SourceBraTS 2018 / Medical Decathlon
Samples484 training volumes
Format4D NIfTI (.nii.gz)
Shape240 × 240 × 155 × 4
Spacing1mm isotropic

Label Mapping

0Background
1Necrotic / non-enhancing core
2Peritumoral edema
3GD-enhancing tumor

Preprocessing

LoadImage(image_only=True)
↓
EnsureChannelFirst()
↓
NormalizeIntensity(
  nonzero=True,
  channel_wise=True
)

Inference

SlidingWindowInferer(
  roi=[240, 240, 160],
  overlap=0.5
)
Post: sigmoid → threshold(0.5)

Confidence

Model Confidence Across Samples & Regions

Key Takeaways

  • All confidence values > 0.88
  • WT consistently highest (avg 0.938)
  • TC second (avg 0.927)
  • ET slightly lower (avg 0.897) — expected, as enhancing regions are harder to delineate
  • BRATS_004 has highest overall confidence (0.952 / 0.962 / 0.900)

Clinical Interpretation

High confidence (>0.9) across regions indicates the model produces reliable segmentation boundaries suitable for clinical review.

Metrics

Per-Sample Dice Scores

Aggregated Dice

RegionDice
Tumor Core (TC)0.8694
Whole Tumor (WT)0.3126
Mean0.5910

Note on WT Score

WT Dice is lower due to label mapping mismatch between Medical Decathlon (labels 0,1,2,3) and the BraTS Challenge format (0,1,2,4) the model was trained on. Fine-tuning on aligned labels will resolve this.

Integration

Detection Adapter — Production Code

SegResNetDetectionAdapter

Drop-in replacement for StubDetectionAdapter. Conforms to DetectionAdapter protocol.

class DetectionAdapter(Protocol):
  def run(
    self,
    input_path: Path,
    workdir: Path,
    request: DetectionRequest
  ) -> Tuple[Path, Path]: ...
adapter = SegResNetDetectionAdapter(
  bundle_dir="model_bundles/
    brats_mri_segmentation",
  device="cuda:0"
)

Pipeline

  • Loads 4-channel NIfTI
  • EnsureChannelFirst + NormalizeIntensity
  • SlidingWindow inference with AMP
  • BraTS-format label map (0/1/2/4)
  • Annotated NIfTI segmentation
  • Structured FindingsDocument JSON
✓
Tests pass
✓
Protocol OK
✓
GPU accel
Infrastructure

Deployment Stack

Docker

Separate containers for reconstruction, detection, pipeline. GPU compose.

FastAPI

/health, /ready, /infer. Async jobs with background workers.

S3 Storage

Object storage for inter-service data. Local fallback for dev.

PostgreSQL

Job tracking, artifacts, state machine. SQLAlchemy ORM.

Hardware

RTX 4090
24GB VRAM
130 GB
System RAM
CUDA 12.1
Compute
Python 3.12
Runtime
Roadmap

Next Steps

Model

  • Fine-tune on Genotek dataset
  • Fix WT label mapping
  • Evaluate SwinUNETR backbone
  • Add Hausdorff distance metric

Pipeline

  • Wire adapter into Container 2
  • End-to-end: k-space → findings
  • DICOM I/O support
  • SSIM/PSNR for reconstruction

Clinical

  • Expert review of segmentation
  • Radiologist feedback on output
  • Edge case analysis
  • Regulatory assessment

Production

  • Load testing
  • Model versioning & A/B
  • Monitoring (Grafana)
  • CI/CD for model updates
